steps.title: Identify the contrasting assessments
steps.content: Text 1 describes the Kolvereid & Moen study as having “convincingly demonstrated” its findings. Text 2 identifies a fatal methodological flaw—lack of pre-training data—making causation “unclear”. The texts reach opposite conclusions about how persuasive the study's evidence is.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content: Choice B directly asks whether the study provides persuasive evidence that the training caused results—the exact point of disagreement. Choices A and D address topics the texts don't explore; choice C states a fact both texts confirm. That matches choice B — B is correct.
